message:
🐛 fix(nodes-drilldown): explain empty list when RBAC blocks node enumeration (#8359)

The all-nodes drill-down reads from `useCachedNodes()` (direct node list
endpoint), while the stat-overview block that opens it sums
`cluster.nodeCount` from the cluster summary. When the current user has
cluster-level read access but lacks `list nodes` RBAC, the overview
shows e.g. "31 nodes" but the drill-down returns 0 records and displayed
a bare "No items found" — contradicting the number the user just
clicked on (#8312).

Surface the discrepancy explicitly:
- While the node list is still loading, show "Loading node details…"
  instead of the misleading empty-state.
- Once loading finishes, if the cluster summaries reported >0 nodes but
  the detail list is empty, show a yellow-flagged info card that
  restates the expected count, distinguishes RBAC-gating from endpoint
  failure (via `isFailed`), and explains why the two views disagree.
- Other views and the generic empty case still render the existing
  "No items found" message.

Fixes #8312

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -158,10 +158,24 @@ function getStatusBadge(status: string) { export function MultiClusterSummaryDrillDown({ data, viewType }: MultiClusterSummaryDrillDownProps) { const { t } = useTranslation() const { clusters, deduplicatedClusters, pods, deployments, events, helmReleases, operatorSubscriptions, securityIssues } = useClusterData() - const { nodes: rawCachedNodes, lastRefresh: nodesLastRefresh } = useCachedNodes() + const { + nodes: rawCachedNodes, + lastRefresh: nodesLastRefresh, + isLoading: nodesIsLoading, + isFailed: nodesIsFailed, + } = useCachedNodes() const { pvcs: cachedPVCs } = useCachedPVCs() // Guard against undefined to prevent crashes when APIs return 404/500/empty const cachedNodes = rawCachedNodes || [] + // For the all-nodes view: the overview stat block sums cluster.nodeCount, + // but the detail list is fetched from a separate endpoint that may return + // empty when the caller lacks list-nodes RBAC on some clusters (#8312). + // Track the expected count so we can explain the discrepancy instead of + // showing a blank "No items found". + const expectedNodeCountFromClusters = (clusters || []).reduce( + (sum, c) => sum + (c.nodeCount || 0), + 0, + ) // Convert epoch ms to ISO string for the freshness indicator const nodesDataAge = (() => { if (!nodesLastRefresh) return null @@ -485,9 +499,35 @@ export function MultiClusterSummaryDrillDown({ data, viewType }: MultiClusterSum {/* Items List */}
{filteredItems.length === 0 ? ( -
- No items found -
+ viewType === 'all-nodes' && nodesIsLoading ? ( +
+ Loading node details… +
+ ) : viewType === 'all-nodes' && + cachedNodes.length === 0 && + expectedNodeCountFromClusters > 0 ? ( +
+
+ +
+
+ Cluster summary reports {expectedNodeCountFromClusters} node + {expectedNodeCountFromClusters === 1 ? '' : 's'}, but the + detailed list is empty. +
+
+ {nodesIsFailed + ? 'The node list endpoint is currently unreachable.' + : 'This usually means the current user lacks list-nodes RBAC on one or more clusters, so the detail view can\'t enumerate nodes even though the per-cluster summary includes their count.'} +
+
+
+
+ ) : ( +
+ No items found +
+ ) ) : ( filteredItems.slice(0, 100).map((item, idx) => { const name = (item as Record)[config.nameKey] as string || (item as Record).name as string || 'Unknown'
